title: Fix client_reference_id for Stripe Purchase Button
story: As a DataTools Pro user purchasing additional licenses, I want the Stripe checkout to correctly identify both my team and user account, so that my purchase is properly attributed and my team's license count is automatically updated.
description:
## Context

The production purchase button was generating an incorrect `client_reference_id` in the Stripe checkout URL. Testing revealed the ID did not match the logged-in user's actual team and user identifiers, breaking the end-to-end purchase flow.

## Problem

The rendered Stripe URL contained an invalid `client_reference_id` value (e.g., `8516F5AD29` instead of the expected `teamId_userId` format). The user and account IDs did not match the logged-in user's actual identifiers.

## Fix

Update the client_reference_id construction to concatenate `teamId_userId` using an underscore delimiter. Both the frontend URL construction and the backend Add Users API endpoint (MBT-454) need to be updated to use this format consistently.

Example corrected URL:
`https://buy.stripe.com/aEU2bI6ST6s7bEQeUV?client_reference_id=8516F5AD2934_8516F5AD2949`
acceptance_criteria: client_reference_id in the Stripe URL follows the format teamId_userId
Team ID and User ID are the correct public-facing unique IDs for the logged-in user
The Add Users API endpoint correctly parses the updated client_reference_id format
End-to-end purchase flow works: click button -> Stripe checkout -> payment -> user count updated
ux_changes: Fixed the Stripe purchase button URL to include the correctly formatted client_reference_id (teamId_userId) for the logged-in user.
middleware_changes: Updated the client_reference_id construction logic to properly concatenate the team's unique ID and user's unique ID with an underscore delimiter.
api_changes: Updated the /api/v1_0/addusers endpoint to parse the new client_reference_id format (teamId_userId) and correctly extract the team identifier.
implementation_notes: The Pendio script (implemented May 2024) had already established the pattern of using visitor ID and account ID. The fix aligned the purchase button's client_reference_id with this same pattern. The underscore delimiter was chosen for concatenation. Both the frontend URL generation and backend API endpoint were updated simultaneously.
comment_summary: Developer confirmed the existing Pendio script pattern and proposed concatenating the team ID. After agreement on using underscore as delimiter, both the client_reference_id and API endpoint were updated. Changes were pushed to staging, reviewed, and deployed to production.
